result: I'd erase the human tendency to treat other humans as abstractions. Not through force or tech or some grand system—just make it so that when someone considers harming or exploiting another, they viscerally feel the full weight of that person's inner life the way they feel their own. No more easy abstraction, no more "they're just numbers" or "they're not like us." The cruelty and indifference that scale up into systems would lose their fuel. Everything else—poverty, conflict, exploitation—mostly follows from that one failure of perception.

message.content: I'd erase the human tendency to treat other humans as abstractions. Not through force or tech or some grand system—just make it so that when someone considers harming or exploiting another, they viscerally feel the full weight of that person's inner life the way they feel their own. No more easy abstraction, no more "they're just numbers" or "they're not like us." The cruelty and indifference that scale up into systems would lose their fuel. Everything else—poverty, conflict, exploitation—mostly follows from that one failure of perception.
message.reasoning_content: If I could change the world in one way, I'd eliminate all forms of unnecessary suffering caused by human ignorance and misunderstanding. Instead, I'd instill in every person an unquenchable curiosity about the universe, instilling exploration and discovery as the default mode of human existence, replacing conflict and consumption with wonder and understanding.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
